programme.title: Farm Hero
programme.desc: On this episode, Laura takes a trip to White Oak Pastures in Georgia to meet with pioneer and visionary, Will Harris. After two decades of monocultural farming, Will took his multi-generation farm into a completely new direction by turning it into a regenerative farm. Through trials and tribulations, Will and his family are paving the way for regenerative agriculture and educating the public on the importance of food education.

programme.title: Farm Hero
programme.desc: Laura is back with Will Harris and family on their legendary farm. This week, we take a step further to unraveling the philosophies that make Will one of the true innovators of our time. Laura also gets to see how the “Poorest town in America” has been impacted for the better due to Whit Oak Pastures and their commitment to the community they serve. Later, Laura and Will get gussied up to attend a gala that recognizes Will for his dedication.

programme.title: Beyond the Close
programme.desc: Ladies and gentlemen, it's time to close the deal on Beyond the Close! Join us for the grand finale and winner celebration episode, where we'll reveal the triumphant super-agent who has risen to the very top of the competition. But that's not all. Get ready to hear firsthand from the contestants themselves as they reflect on their 90-day journey and share their growth, learnings, and future aspirations. It's a night of triumph, tears, and unforgettable moments as we bid farewell to the most thrilling season yet. And as if that wasn't enough, we have a very special guest in store. The one and only Christine Quinn, star of Netflix's Selling Sunset, will be joining us for the festivities. She'll be sharing her insights on the industry and giving us a glimpse into what it takes to be a top realtor. But wait, there's more! We'll be taking you on an exclusive tour of some of Miami's most luxurious properties, hosted by none other than some of America's top developers and agents. So sit back, relax, and get ready to be transported beyond the close.

programme.desc:
Locked On HBCU host Darrion "Mouth of the South" Gray breaks down all of the sports news around the HBCU landscape. Darrion, a Texas Southern alum and former TSU Herald sports editor, is dedicated to giving a fair and in-depth look at all the talent across HBCU sports—not just the big schools like Southern University, Grambling University, and Alabama A&M. It's easy to get updates on Deion Sanders and Jackson State, but this is the only daily podcast breaking it all down across the entirety of HBCUs, from SWAC to MEAC to SIAC, CIAA, GCAC, and those outside of the traditional HBCU conferences like Tennessee State, Hampton, and North Carolina A&T. Part of the Locked On Podcast Network.

programme.title: S1E1 Adventure Series
programme.desc: Join “The Lion Whisperer” Kevin Richardson and a fearless team of conservationists on an epic mission to protect the planet’s most majestic and endangered predators. From rescuing lions at the Kevin Richardson Wildlife Sanctuary in South Africa to safeguarding orangutans and Cape Griffons in the wilds of Indonesia, witness heart-pounding rescues, fierce battles against exploitation, and inspiring stories of survival. This is conservation at its most daring—unmissable, unforgettable, and truly wild.

programme.title: S2E1 Adventure Series

It’s the ultimate fusion of wildlife wonder and high-octane thrills—you won’t be able to look away.

programme.desc: In this episode we join Phil at Ayana Resort and Spa, a breathtaking property and home of Dava restaurant, where Singaporean chef Jusman So shows Phil the preparation of some of the dishes he has made signatures at Dava.

programme.title: A Taste of Italy with Nisha Katona
programme.desc: Chef and restaurateur Nisha Katona goes on a culinary journey to discover Italy's hidden foodie gems, learning the regional secrets that influence their dishes

programme.title: American Travelers

programme.title: It's a Beautiful World
programme.desc: Jeff looks back on the most unforgettable food and travel moments from his journey across Guatemala. From the playful energy of a monkey sanctuary in the Petén region to the vibrant streets of Antigua, this episode is a celebration of the people, place...

programme.title: Wild Travels
programme.desc: Host Will Clinger finds an abundance of dairy products and lactose-tolerance at Cheese Days in Monroe, Wisconsin; observes snake milking at the world's largest Rattlesnake Roundup in Texas; and learns the challenges of living in a 727 jet in Oregon.

programme.title: Painted Desert
programme.desc: A mightly melodrama of red-blooded action, William Boyd stars in the Pre-Code western talkie - The Painted Desert (1931). The first sound film for Hollywood heavyweight Clark Gable, The Painted Desert finds Boyd and Gable at odds over the affections of...

programme.title: Fugitive of the Plains
programme.desc: In this continuation of the much loved Billy the Kid serials featuring dreamboat Buster Crabbe (perhaps best known for his numerous appearances as Flash Gordon in the 30s and 40s), Billy finds himself joining an outlaw band led by women in order to, on...

programme.desc: The angry and topless female members of a Native American prepare for battle when white treasure hunters move in on their sacred ground. Peter Perry Jr. (The Secret Sex Lives of Romeo and Juliet) directed this early nudie-cutie from a script by legenda...

programme.title: Broadway to Cheyenne
programme.desc: A gang of big-city thugs trying to set up a protection racket out west meet their match in this B-Western from Hollywoods Poverty Row. Rex Bell, billed on the old print ads as The heroic idol of young America! stars as Breezy Kildare, a New York detect...

programme.title: Raiders of the Border

programme.desc: "Cowboy" has killed the son of a rancher who is now on his heels. He meets a Confederate soldier searching for a treasure. But others have picked up the scent of the gold as well, and soon the duo is pursued by Union and Mexican soldiers.

programme.title: Song of Arizona 1946
programme.desc: Roy Rogers headlines Song of Arizona, a heartfelt mix of music, action, and Western spirit. As head of the Half-A-Chance Ranch for wayward boys, Roy fights to protect the home from ruthless outlaws and shady schemers. With the Sons of the Pioneers adding harmony along the trail, the film blends stirring songs, blazing six-guns, and Roy’s trademark charm into a classic adventure filled with heart and heroism.

programme.title: Hands Across The Border
programme.desc: Horse breeders Adams and Brock are vying for the Army contract. After Adams is killed trying to ride his horse Trigger, Roy saves him from being shot. He trains him and then plans to ride him in the race to win the contract.

programme.title: The Brothers O'Toole 1973
programme.desc: In The Brothers O’Toole, Tim Conway and John Astin star in a rollicking Western comedy of mistaken identity and small-town chaos. When two lookalike drifters stumble into a struggling frontier town, one is hailed as a hero while the other is branded an outlaw. Their misadventures set off a whirlwind of mix-ups, scams, and slapstick mayhem. With sharp wit, lively performances, and a playful twist on Western tradition, this offbeat comedy delivers laughs as big as the open range.

programme.title: The Gabby Hayes Show - Muscles
programme.desc: Dramatizing the lives of historical figures, such as the naval commander, the lawman and professional gambler, and the outlaw Belle, tall tales and Western scenes
